data.body:
PLAN REVIEW · PASS 1 · [G6] Approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection — VERDICT: CLEAN (no findings).

Grounded the correctness reasoning in the live eval seam (src/rebar/llm/eval.py) and the existing packaged specs (src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/), plus both children (rink-cloud-gum/b4af, seek-ditch-corgi/4024).

MECHANISM CORRECTNESS (PASS):
- Two-tier eval model (deterministic gate every PR at zero API cost + LLM-judge tier off the PR critical path) is directly enforced by the existing discipline: validate_eval_spec requires ≥1 DETERMINISTIC gating scorer (eval.py:219-220) and llm-judge scorers must carry gates:false (validate_scorer eval.py:124-127, "judges REPORT"). The offline discipline (validate_eval_spec/at_least_passes/coverage/cohens_kappa/to_junit) is genuinely stdlib-only/offline-testable (eval.py:19-21), so the "no model call on every PR" tier is achievable as written.
- Scorer registry (AC "every scorer name maps to an implemented pure function; validate_scorer rejects an unregistered name") correctly targets a real gap: today validate_scorer only checks a deterministic scorer HAS a name (eval.py:120-123) — names are inert. The proposed registry + rejection is the right closure of that hole.
- run_eval() wiring (AC "no longer raises NotImplementedError") is the seam's stated intended completion: the Inspect Task is assembled from the already-validated spec (eval.py:341-345, currently NotImplementedError at 346-351). Sound.
- Context-injection solver for the 3 store/repo-coupled agentic reviewers (AC5) is structurally correct: these reviewers read the store+repo via tools at runtime, so a static inline input would be unfaithful; context-injection + stubbed/fixture-backed repo tools preserves production fidelity and remains deterministic for CI.
- eval-live rails (workflow_dispatch + weekly, continue-on-error, response-cached, concurrency-capped, no live call on fork PRs/Dependabot) are standard, correct hardening for a credentialed live-LLM job.

FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE (PASS): the success criterion ("a prompt edit is regression-gated by an eval that actually SCORES the output, not one that only shape-validates the spec") solves the named problem rather than a proxy — it directly addresses the current "validated for shape but never scored" state.

APPROACH SELECTION (PASS): generated two structurally-different alternatives and discarded both as inferior — (a) single-tier "gate everything including the LLM judge on every PR" is rejected by the codebase's own hard rule that judge non-determinism must not create false confidence; (b) inline-feeding the agentic reviewers a captured transcript instead of a context-injection solver is less faithful to production tool-reading behavior. The chosen approach is at-least-as-good on codebase-alignment, blast-radius, testability, simplicity, and robustness. (Alternatives intentionally NOT written into the plan.)

POSITIVE RATIONALE (PASS, borderline): the why-this-architecture rationale lives mainly in child b4af ("regression-gated... not a blind cutover"; "cheap deterministic gate every PR + expensive LLM-judge tier OFF the PR critical path") rather than in the epic body's own prose, which is thinner here. Per pass-1 decisiveness guidance, the plan + its linked child affirmatively carry the rationale, so this PASSES rather than producing a manufactured MINOR finding. (Not emitted as a finding.)

AFFIRMATIONS: mechanism achieves the goal; solves the named problem not a proxy; chosen approach defensible vs generated alternatives; positive rationale present (via the child).

Empty findings list for G6.
